behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-to-hook-input handoff, not just raw JSON decode. The behavior text says "parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling," which requires wiring multiple local modules. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required by this behavior text; an in-process integration test can catch parser/handler boundary failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test through the real hook-input pipeline modules (JSON parse + downstream input handling) using valid payloads with code fences, backticks, and $vars inside string values; assert successful parse and normal continuation state.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on interaction between parser failure and trace-record construction/context capture. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real process I/O, only that malformed payloads map to a diagnostic trace record; this can be verified with in-process module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Inject malformed JSON strings into the in-process hook input path and assert a `json_parse_failed` trace record is emitted with diagnostic fields (e.g., error message/location/snippet) sufficient to identify malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: This is a local policy-mapping rule: given parse failure input, return handled result and telemetry intent without throwing. Failure is within one local function/object boundary. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly says "local error-policy path," which disqualifies needing multi-module infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy mapper directly with a parse-failure object and assert it returns handled/non-throw outcome plus telemetry-intent marker.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "real process execution" and malformed stdin handling with crash/exit semantics; this is subprocess + OS I/O boundary. Mocking would hide process-lifecycle failures.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook process as a real subprocess, pipe malformed stdin, wait for exit, assert no crash behavior (controlled exit) and presence of expected trace signal before process termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text requires a "real hook invocation path" including stdin and trace artifact emission, which crosses process/OS I/O boundaries. In-process fakes could miss real invocation and sink integration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook via its real invocation entrypoint, provide malformed stdin, and verify end-to-end trace artifact output from parser failure to actual trace sink.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: This regression depends on comparing outcomes across parser + failure-classification/trace handling paths for two input classes (valid markdown-containing JSON vs malformed JSON). Rejected System because behavior text is about attribution logic distinction, not real subprocess/OS behavior; in-process module wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Build paired in-process cases: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings and (b) malformed JSON. Assert only malformed case yields parse-failure classification/trace, while valid markdown case follows success path.
